title: OCI registry support and listing Helm Charts
body:
I want to list all Helm charts available in a specific Helm repository in an OCI registry.

- Running `skopeo list-tags oci://<Helm_registry>/<Helm_Repo>/<Helm_Chart>` errors with `FATA[0000] Unsupported transport 'oci' for tag listing. Only supported: docker, docker-archive`
- Running `skopeo list-tags oci:<Helm_registry>/<Helm_Repo>/<Helm_Chart>` errors with `FATA[0000] Unsupported transport 'oci' for tag listing. Only supported: docker, docker-archive`
- Running `skopeo list-tags <Helm_registry>/<Helm_Repo>/<Helm_Chart>` errors with `FATA[0000] Invalid "<URL>": does not specify a transport`
- It seems `docker://` works with this particular Helm Registry, but I'm not sure if this will work with all OCI registries. I don't want to use `docker://`; I want to use the `oci://` transport.

[Here](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/docs/skopeo.1.md) it says that oci and oci-archive should be available, but my skopeo version doesn't seem to have them (installed via `dnf install skopeo`) and I also don't know the proper syntax how to write the URL.

**OS:** RHEL 9.5
**skopeo version:** 1.16.1

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

> * It seems `docker://` works with this particular Helm Registry, but I'm not sure if this will work with all OCI registries. I don't want to use `docker://`; I want to use the `oci://` transport.

That’s not what the transport names mean for Skopeo. `docker://` means “using the docker/distribution ~= opencontainers/distribution-spec protocol to talk to a remote registry”, there is ~no functional difference between “Docker registries” and “OCI registries”. `oci:` means “local files using the  opencontainers/image-spec file layout”.

(Yes, I’m afraid that can be rather confusing; there’s been an attempt to support a `registry:` alias for the `docker:` transport, but that ended up being too complex to implement without side effects.)

Using `docker://` is correct and appropriate in this case, and it is expected to work long-term.

Hi @mtrmac

I see. Okay, thanks for the info.

Also it seems that an OCI registry can contain container images, helm charts, arbitrary files etc. It's hard to know whether what you get in return is only Helm charts.
